response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development across multiple languages and domains, but lacks direct experience with C++ and industrial automation systems. Their background is heavily focused on web and mobile development, with only limited exposure to C/C++ (e.g., National Instruments LabWindows/CVI) and no mention of STL, secure development lifecycle, or IEC 62443 standards. The role requires hands-on C++ development for high-performance, networked systems in industrial automation, which this resume does not strongly align with.
strengths: Strong problem-solving and software craftsmanship skills with experience in multiple languages and paradigms
Proven ability to deliver scalable solutions (e.g., event-driven platforms, real-time systems)
Experience with secure development practices (TDD, CI/CD, code quality standards)
concerns: Limited to no experience with C++ and STL, which is core to the role
No demonstrated expertise in industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) or secure development lifecycle per industrial standards
Lacks background in industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.) or cloud services relevant to the role
